trials.supervisor_analysis: Stage 1 baseline_profile is complete. Baseline established at 5.36ms (score 50-60). MIOpen assembly conv dominates 100% of GPU time. Multiple approaches tried with key learnings about ROCm limitations. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: Writing a correct 2D conv Triton kernel from scratch is very hard. First establish a working baseline by having `ModelNew.forward()` simply call `torch.nn.functional.conv2d` — this will give you a baseline score. Then try `torch.compile` on that. Only after you have baseline numbers should you attempt a custom Triton kernel. You can also consider the im2col + triton matmul approach which is much easier to get correct than a direct conv kernel.

Read the test harness (`cat /workspace/test_harness.py`) to understand how weights are transferred from `Model` to `ModelNew`. Typically, the harness copies the state_dict — so your `ModelNew` must have parameters with the same names/shapes as `Model`. The simplest correct baseline is to have `ModelNew` contain the exact same `self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(...)` and `forward` method as `Model`.

Try converting the input and weight tensors to channels-last memory format (`x.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last)`) before the conv2d call — this often gives significant speedup for convolutions on AMD GPUs since the underlying MIOpen kernels are optimized for NHWC layout. Also make the conv2d module itself channels-last with `self.conv2d = self.conv2d.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last)`.
